key_insight: MTP acceptance during RL is constrained by entropy fluctuation, and probabilistic rejection sampling plus an end-to-end TV loss can restore high acceptance rates and speed up rollouts.
engineering_relevance: The paper offers a practical recipe for accelerating RL training pipelines in large language models, improving end-to-end throughput without requiring costly online MTP updates.
method: Analyze how RL-induced entropy growth lowers MTP acceptance rates.
Use probabilistic rejection sampling instead of greedy draft sampling to reduce entropy disturbance.
Train with an end-to-end TV loss that directly optimizes multi-step acceptance rate.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and agentic tasks, the method reports about 10% higher acceptance rates, up to 95% acceptance, up to 25% extra inference throughput, and up to 1.8x end-to-end async RL acceleration on Qwen3.5/3.6/3.7.
watch_item: The gains depend on careful integration of rejection sampling and TV-loss MTP pretraining, so deployment may require additional tuning for different model sizes and RL setups.
paper_title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
